summary: Iranian forces have deployed a modified Ketral/Catral loitering munition with an X-shaped tail fin and rear rocket booster, part of a new group of miniature attack drones. Ukrainian forces struck Russian positions in Kursk, destroying FPV launch sites, depots, and shelters, while Russian Army aviation hit a Ukrainian UAV launch point in Kharkiv using LMUR. Russian units are actively intercepting Ukrainian drones with Elka and Yolka anti-drone systems, with operators achieving clear communication during engagements. In Iraq, pro-Iranian groups used fiber-optic FPV drones to surveil the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, and Iranian-backed militias attacked the U.S. Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport. Ukrainian forces are experimenting with tactics like dismounting riders before FPV strikes, and both sides continue to suffer losses to drone attacks, including a Russian soldier killed by two simultaneous FPV strikes.
